patterns.label: Azure DevOps Eval Work-Item Notification Triage
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 52 confirmed freq=30. Work-item types: Task, Requirement, Bug, Feature, Epic. PM is subscriber/mentioned rather than direct assignee — pure routing overhead. 960 cumulative occurrences across 32 active cycles. Highest automation score in catalog (95). Decomposition into workstream sub-classifiers overdue.

patterns.label: Recurring Meeting Agenda Preparation (12 Confirmed Types)
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 52 confirmed: Weekly Evals Sync (Tue 3:05), Project Crucible v-Team (Mon 8:05), Manager-Led Team Meeting (Mon 11:05), Solution Library Check-In (Mon 9:05), Golden Evals Bi-Weekly (Thu), Learning Fridays (Fri 2:05). All extended through August with locked slots and stable attendees. 416 cumulative occurrences across 32 cycles.

patterns.label: Meeting-to-Loop-Notes-to-Follow-Up Workflow
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 52 confirmed via two signals: Meeting-to-Notes-to-Follow-Up Workflow (freq=4, 30 min) and Loop Meeting Notes Creation (freq=4, 40 min). Consistent structure: Agenda → Notes → Decisions/Open Questions → Follow-up tasks. MCS Analytics CAPECAT-PM Review 2026-03-11 is archetypal. 256 cumulative occurrences across 32 cycles.

patterns.label: Inbound Eval Strategy & Tooling Expert Coaching
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 52 confirmed: Inbound Eval Strategy Q&A (freq=10, 60 min) + Eval Tooling CLI Troubleshooting (freq=5, 30 min). PM answers: how many eval cases are enough?, what metrics to use (groundedness, Compare Meaning, tool_call_accuracy)?, CSV import quirks, feature flags. BSI-critical bottleneck — PM is sole DRI for 32 cycles. 480 cumulative occurrences.

patterns.label: Security and Compliance Alert Review
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 52 confirmed: 7 distinct alerts (Action Required: File Blocked, elevated/revoked Administrator, Loop workspace attention) from Office365/Purview, Open Source Notifications, Microsoft Digital Compliance. Alert subjects follow rigid patterns — classifier handles auto-triage, escalation, and noise suppression. 224 cumulative occurrences.

patterns.label: Viva Engage and Community Digest Consumption
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 52 confirmed freq=5: Microsoft Daily Digest, Weekly Digest, community announcements (Copilot Tasks, Copilot Champs, Immigration Q&A, SharePoint AI tips). Passive recipient — no action typically required. Classifier scans subjects/bodies, extracts relevant signal, suppresses noise. 160 cumulative occurrences.

patterns.label: Multi-Day Training Event and Conference Coordination
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 52 confirmed: Camp AIR multi-day training event (900 min) plus EvalCon content planning discussions (freq=4, 40 min). Event occurs periodically (~every 6 cycles). Coordinator skill handles standup agendas, EOD debriefs, deadline tracking, and leadership alignment drafts. 64 cumulative occurrences (periodic cadence).

patterns.label: External Partner and Customer Eval Sessions (50+ Domains)
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 52 confirmed: 6 external meetings, 780 min across 50+ external domains (Accenture 2h, HCLTech 1h50m, EY 55m, KPMG 3h, Avanade 1h, Cognizant 1h). Highest cumulative hours pattern at 416 hrs. Pre-session context packs and post-session recaps repeat structurally per partner type. 192 cumulative occurrences.

patterns.label: Parallel Eval Enablement Deck and Template Creation
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 52 confirmed freq=3 (90 min): TFM Agent Evals, CAPE_EVALs_Playbook_v5, eval convo decks, execution plans — created by different team members with overlapping content. Team meetings explicitly flag version confusion. Scaffolder skill standardizes into canonical pipeline. 256 cumulative occurrences across 32 cycles.

patterns.label: Skill-Detector Technical Spec and JSON Config Authoring
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 52 confirmed freq=5 (60 min): ZIP packages with SKILL.md specs, .sh scripts, .json config, .db files produced in short intervals. skill-detector.zip and skill-detector 1.zip observed. Boilerplate structure is highly consistent — generator skill scaffolds these artifacts from natural-language descriptions. 160 cumulative occurrences.

patterns.label: Daily Personal Day-Prep Planning Page
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 52 confirmed freq=5 (25 min): Files named '[Mar 19] Help me prepare for my day' — structured morning planning ritual. PM updates a consistent artifact rather than starting from scratch. An M365-aware briefing skill auto-composes the daily prep page from calendar, email, and Teams context each morning. 160 cumulative occurrences.

patterns.label: Technical Artifact Distribution via Teams
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 52 confirmed freq=8 (25 min): PM shares GitHub repos, ZIP files, YAML, Loop components, SharePoint links via Teams. Commonly shared: Copilot SDK, skills agents, templates, playbooks, PRDs, demo ZIPs. Bundler skill auto-packages context-enriched artifact links with standardized role-based descriptions. 256 cumulative occurrences.

patterns.label: Agent Architecture and MCP/Skills Scoping Discussions
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 52 confirmed: Agent Architecture and MCP/Skills Scoping (freq=5, 35 min) + Urgent Technical Coordination Declarative Agent (freq=1, 25 min). Topics: MCP servers, CLI vs UI tradeoffs, WorkIQ constraints, skill-creator, PRD generator, declarative agents grounded in 170+ SharePoint sites. Discovery skill answers scoping questions from skills taxonomy knowledge base. 192 cumulative occurrences.

patterns.label: Weekly Status Update Reporting (Multi-Format Parallel Effort)
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 52 confirmed freq=1 (45 min): Teams restate same progress in 3+ concurrent formats — weekly sync done/next/blockers, bi-weekly newsletter, Cape Day/LT slides. Status generator skill produces all output formats from a single input, eliminating redundant re-authoring. 32 cumulative occurrences.

patterns.label: CAPE and Evals Cross-Org Leadership Strategy Threads
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 52 confirmed freq=5 (1.58 hrs): CAPE East Project Crucible Deepdive, Customer donated evals Playbook, EvalCon announcement threads. Spans 5 leadership roles across 3 sub-types: strategic alignment, knowledge transfer, urgent technical escalation. Synthesis and decision capture are structured and repeatable. 160 cumulative occurrences.

patterns.label: High-Urgency Escalation and External Follow-Up Email Authoring
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 52 confirmed freq=2 (45 min): external administrative escalation (URGENT degree cannot be located, external university) and urgent technical escalation (URGENT DBS Declarative Agent, SharePoint grounding). Two urgency tiers with distinct tone and framing — learnable patterns applicable across similar scenarios. 64 cumulative occurrences.

patterns.label: Cross-Org VOC Routing and DRI Navigation Assistance
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 52 confirmed freq=4 (20 min): PM acts as informal routing layer from Field Engineers to MCS Analytics, Eval team, and PG Team — answering 'who owns this?' four times/week. VOC routing skill with current DRI mappings could fully deflect these queries. 128 cumulative occurrences across 32 cycles.

patterns.label: Weekly Solution Library Intake and Progress Check-In
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 52 confirmed freq=1 (25 min): Monday 9:05–9:30 AM recurring slot focused on solution intake and progress tracking. Intake criteria and progress fields follow consistent schema — QA assistant pre-processes submissions before the meeting. Lowest composite score in catalog; lower automation complexity limits priority. 32 cumulative occurrences.

patterns.label: Eval Enablement Deck Proliferation and Version Conflict
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 52 confirmed freq=3 (90 min): TFM Agent Evals, Evaluation.pptx, CAPE_EVALs_Playbook_v5 created by different team members with overlapping content. Team meetings flag confusion from multiple floating versions. Overlaps significantly with parallel-eval-artifact-creation — consolidation of these two patterns is recommended next cycle. 96 cumulative occurrences.

patterns.label: Loop Workspace Collaboration Invite Dispatch
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 52 confirmed freq=2 (5 min): PM manually initiates Loop workspace collaborations — 'You have been looped in!' invites for Skilluminator Workspace. Low time cost per occurrence but accumulates. A skill could auto-provision and dispatch invites when new collaboration context is detected from calendar or Teams activity. 64 cumulative occurrences.

candidateSkills.name: ado-notification-router
candidateSkills.description: Classifies Azure DevOps Eval work-item notifications by type (Task, Requirement, Bug, Feature, Epic), workstream, and PM action-required status. Routes actionable items to a triage queue and silences FYI-only noise. Highest automation score in catalog (95). 960 occurrences across 32 active cycles — DEEP SATURATION.
triggerExamples: Triage today's ADO inbox — what actually needs my response?
Classify all Eval notifications by workstream and urgency level
Which Bug notifications are blocking vs. informational this week?
Route Task ####### to the correct DRI based on workstream
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Eliminates ~24 hrs of passive triage overhead. Automation score 95 — highest in catalog. 960 occurrences. DECOMPOSE into workstream sub-classifiers for 5x additional savings. Est. ~$63K/yr direct; ~$315K/yr decomposed.

candidateSkills.name: recurring-agenda-generator
candidateSkills.description: Generates pre-meeting agendas for 12 confirmed recurring meeting types based on fixed time slots, stable attendee sets, previous-meeting action items, and current-week context from Loop and Teams. Covers full Monday–Friday operating rhythm calendar including partner reviews, evals syncs, and learning blocks.
triggerExamples: Prepare the agenda for today's Project Crucible v-team sync
Generate the Monday operating rhythm agenda package for all 3 morning meetings
Create the Golden Evals bi-weekly review brief with open action items
Draft the Learning Fridays session outline from last week's follow-ups
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Reclaims ~304 hrs cumulative meeting prep overhead across 416 occurrences. Covers 12 meeting types extended through August. Est. ~$790K/yr at current cadence.

candidateSkills.name: eval-design-advisor
candidateSkills.description: Codifies the PM's eval expertise to answer recurring field questions autonomously: eval case count heuristics, metric selection (groundedness, Compare Meaning, tool_call_accuracy), rubric design, CSV import/export quirks, feature flag status, CLI vs in-product tradeoffs, and eval-driven development methodology. Directly addresses the BSI-critical Eval SME bottleneck.
triggerExamples: How many eval cases do I need for a 3-intent declarative agent?
Should I use keyword match or semantic grading for this customer scenario?
CSV import is failing — what are the known formatting quirks?
When in the agent lifecycle should evals run for a KPMG deployment?
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Unblocks BSI-critical single point of failure. PM sole DRI for 32 cycles. 480 occurrences, 48 hrs. Frees PM bandwidth for strategic work. Est. ~$125K/yr codification value.

candidateSkills.name: meeting-notes-action-extractor
candidateSkills.description: Ingests meeting transcript, agenda, or Loop document and emits structured output: action items with owner/due-date, decisions log, open questions list, and follow-up email draft. Validated Loop doc structure (Agenda → Notes → Decisions → Follow-up) appears consistently post-meeting across all meeting types.
triggerExamples: Extract action items and decisions from today's Project Crucible v-team sync
Summarize Camp AIR hackathon debrief into a Loop page with owner assignments
Create standardized 1:1 notes with open questions and next steps
Generate post-meeting recap from the CAPE Architecture review
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Replaces ~37 hrs cumulative capture work. 256 occurrences across 32 cycles. Chain head for downstream follow-up and reporting skills. Est. ~$97K/yr.

candidateSkills.name: eval-template-scaffolder
candidateSkills.description: Generates canonical eval enablement artifacts — decks, playbooks, scenario templates, and execution plans — from a customer/partner context input. Eliminates the parallel creation pattern where PM, Program Manager, and Field Engineer independently produce overlapping materials. Provides a diff-aware merge view when existing versions are detected.
triggerExamples: Generate an eval intro deck for a KPMG financial services agent deployment
Scaffold an Agent Eval Sets template for a 5-intent customer agent
Consolidate our 3 floating playbook versions into one canonical CAPE_EVALs_Playbook
Create an eval execution plan from the customer's stated scenarios
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Eliminates ~85 hrs of team-wide duplication. 256 occurrences. Prevents org confusion from version fragmentation. Covers both parallel-eval-artifact-creation and redundant-deck-creation patterns. Est. ~$222K/yr.
